src: καὶ οὐ μὴ πιστεύσητε ἐπὶ τὴν ζωὴν ὑμῶν.
lit: And you should certainly not believe upon your life.
sm: And you should certainly not trust in your life.

syn.mc: Quoted clause continuing the prophetic warning.
syn.svo: Subject: implied ὑμεῖς. Verb: πιστεύσητε. Negation: οὐ μὴ. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τὴν ζωὴν ὑμῶν.
syn.rhet: The quotation keeps the threatening tone before the scriptural exempla begin.

src: ὃ δὲ Δαυεὶδ εἶπεν"
lit: But what David said
sm: But what David said:
emd: Removed trailing OCR quotation mark; no lexical change.

syn.mc: Citation formula introducing the Psalm quotation.
syn.svo: Subject: Δαυεὶδ. Verb: εἶπεν. Relative object: ὃ.
syn.rhet: The formula shifts from warning speech to scriptural proof.

src: Ἵνα τί ἐφρύαξαν ἔθνη
lit: Why did nations rage
sm: Why did the nations rage

syn.mc: Quoted interrogative clause.
syn.svo: Verb: ἐφρύαξαν. Subject: ἔθνη. The opening Ἵνα τί functions as an interrogative formula.
syn.rhet: The Psalm opens with a confrontational question.

src: καὶ λαοὶ ἐμελέτησαν κενά;
lit: And peoples meditated vain things?
sm: And peoples plotted vain things?

syn.mc: Second interrogative clause in the Psalm quotation.
syn.svo: Subject: λαοὶ. Verb: ἐμελέτησαν. Object: κενά.
syn.rhet: Parallelism between nations and peoples deepens the psalmic indictment.

src: παρέστησαν οἱ βασιλεῖς τῆς γῆς
lit: The kings of the earth took their stand
sm: The kings of the earth took their stand

syn.mc: Quoted main clause.
syn.svo: Subject: οἱ βασιλεῖς τῆς γῆς. Verb: παρέστησαν.
syn.rhet: The psalm narrows from peoples to rulers.

src: καὶ οἱ ἄρχοντες συνήχθησαν ἐπὶ τὸ αὐτὸ
lit: And the rulers were gathered together at the same place
sm: And the rulers gathered together

syn.mc: Coordinate clause in the Psalm quotation.
syn.svo: Subject: οἱ ἄρχοντες. Verb: συνήχθησαν. Idiomatic phrase: ἐπὶ τὸ αὐτό = together.
syn.rhet: The social range of opposition is fully assembled.

src: κατὰ τοῦ κυρίου καὶ κατὰ τοῦ χριστοῦ αὐτοῦ ὃ
ana: κατὰ τοῦ κυρίου καὶ κατὰ τοῦ χριστοῦ αὐτοῦ
lit: Against the Lord and against his Christ
sm: against the Lord and against his Christ
emd: Removed stray terminal ὃ, which is OCR debris and syntactically impossible here.

syn.mc: Prepositional continuation of the Psalm citation.
syn.svo: Prepositional objects: τοῦ κυρίου, τοῦ χριστοῦ αὐτοῦ.
syn.rhet: The psalmic indictment culminates in a double object of revolt.

src: ὁ δὲ ᾿Ιερεμίας"
lit: But Jeremiah
sm: But Jeremiah:
emd: Normalized stray initial psili/apostrophe and removed trailing OCR quotation mark.

syn.mc: Citation formula introducing Jeremiah.
syn.svo: Subject-like citation label: ὁ ... Ἰερεμίας.
syn.rhet: The proof text sequence moves from Psalms to Jeremiah.

src: Eγὼ ὡς ἄρνιον ἄκακον ἀγόμενον τοῦ θύεσθαι.
ana: Ἐγὼ ὡς ἄρνιον ἄκακον ἀγόμενον τοῦ θύεσθαι.
tok: pres mp ptcp acc sg n
to be sacrificed; to be slaughtered
lit: I [was] like a harmless lamb being led to be slaughtered.
sm: I was like a harmless lamb being led to be slaughtered.
emd: Normalized mixed Latin/Greek initial E to Greek Ἐ; no other change.

syn.mc: Quoted comparative statement.
syn.sub: The articular infinitive τοῦ θύεσθαι supplies purpose or intended outcome.
syn.svo: Subject: Ἐγὼ. Comparative phrase: ὡς ἄρνιον ἄκακον. Participle: ἀγόμενον. Infinitive: θύεσθαι.
syn.ptc: ἀγόμενον is circumstantial, describing the lamb’s condition.
syn.rhet: The lamb image resumes the sacrificial typology.

src: ἐλογίσαντο εἰς ἐμὲ κακὰ εἰπόντες"
tok: aor act ptcp nom pl m
lit: They devised evil things against me, having said
sm: They devised evil against me, saying
emd: Removed trailing OCR quotation mark; no lexical change.

syn.mc: Main clause with a participial expansion.
syn.sub: The participle εἰπόντες introduces the quoted content that follows implicitly.
syn.svo: Subject: they implied. Verb: ἐλογίσαντο. Object: κακά. Prepositional phrase: εἰς ἐμέ.
syn.ptc: εἰπόντες is circumstantial and introduces speech.
syn.rhet: The quotation shifts from image to hostile intent.

src: Δεῦτε ἐμβάλωμεν ξύλον εἰς τὸν ἄρτον αὐτοῦ
tok: let us cast; let us put
lit: Come, let us cast wood into his bread
sm: Come, let us put wood into his bread

syn.mc: Hortatory quoted clause.
syn.svo: Lead-in: Δεῦτε. Verb: ἐμβάλωμεν. Object: ξύλον. Goal phrase: εἰς τὸν ἄρτον αὐτοῦ.
syn.rhet: The taunt turns into a violent, almost proverbial command.

src: καὶ ἐκτρίψωμεν αὐτὸν ἐϊς γῆς ζώντων"
ana: καὶ ἐκτρίψωμεν αὐτὸν ἐκ γῆς ζώντων
tok: pres act ptcp gen pl m/n
lit: And let us destroy him from the land of the living
sm: And let us destroy him from the land of the living
emd: Corrected syntactically impossible ἐϊς to ἐκ on the standard quotation.

syn.mc: Coordinate hortatory clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied we. Verb: ἐκτρίψωμεν. Object: αὐτόν. Prepositional phrase: ἐκ γῆς ζώντων.
syn.rhet: The command escalates from insertion of wood to total erasure.

src: καὶ τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ οὐ μὴ μνησθῇ"
ana: καὶ τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ οὐ μὴ μνησθῇ
lit: And let his name surely not be remembered
sm: And let his name never be remembered
emd: Removed trailing OCR quotation mark; no lexical change.

syn.mc: Final negative jussive clause of the citation.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ. Verb: μνησθῇ. Strong negation: οὐ μὴ.
syn.rhet: The quotation closes with total obliteration of memory.

src: ὁ δὲ ᾿Ησαΐας"
lit: But Isaiah
sm: But Isaiah:
emd: Normalized stray initial psili/apostrophe and removed trailing OCR quotation mark.

syn.mc: Citation formula introducing Isaiah.
syn.svo: Subject-like citation label: ὁ ... Ἠσαΐας.
syn.rhet: The proof text sequence moves from Jeremiah to Isaiah.

src: 'ῆς πρόβατον εἰς σφαγὴν ἤχθη,
lit: As a sheep he was led to slaughter,
sm: Like a sheep, he was led to slaughter,
emd: Corrected corrupted opening `'ῆς` to Ὡς on contextual grounds.

syn.mc: Quoted comparative clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied servant figure. Verb: ἤχθη. Comparative predicate: Ὡς πρόβατον. Goal phrase: εἰς σφαγήν.
syn.rhet: The Isaiah citation reactivates the sheep image with stronger sacrificial force.

src: καὶ ὡς ἀμνὸς ἄφωνος ἐναντίον τοῦ κείραντος αὐτὸν
tok: aor act ptcp gen sg m
lit: And like a speechless lamb before the one shearing him
sm: And like a silent lamb before the one shearing him

syn.mc: Coordinate comparative clause continuing the Isaiah citation.
syn.sub: The articular participle τοῦ κείραντος αὐτόν is a genitive phrase dependent on ἐναντίον.
syn.svo: Implied subject: the servant/lamb. Comparative phrase: ὡς ἀμνός ἄφωνος. Prepositional phrase: ἐναντίον ... αὐτόν.
syn.ptc: κείραντος is substantival and specifies the shearer.
syn.rhet: The image tightens from slaughter to mute submission.

src: οὗτος οὐκ ἀνοίγει τὸ στόμα αὐτοῦ"
ana: οὗτος οὐκ ἀνοίγει τὸ στόμα αὐτοῦ
lit: This one does not open his mouth
sm: This one does not open his mouth
emd: Removed trailing OCR quotation mark; no lexical change.

syn.mc: Main clause within the Isaiah citation.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτος. Verb: ἀνοίγει. Object: τὸ στόμα αὐτοῦ.
syn.rhet: Silence functions as the climactic mark of innocence.

src: τὴν δὲ γενεὰν αὐτοῦ τίς διηγήσεται"
ana: τὴν δὲ γενεὰν αὐτοῦ τίς διηγήσεται
lit: But who will recount his generation
sm: And who will recount his generation?
emd: Removed trailing OCR quotation mark; no lexical change.

syn.mc: Interrogative clause completing the Isaiah citation.
syn.svo: Interrogative subject: τίς. Verb: διηγήσεται. Object: τὴν γενεὰν αὐτοῦ.
syn.rhet: The rhetorical question broadens the mystery beyond speechless suffering.

src: πολλὰ μὲν οὖν καὶ ἕτερα ὑπὸ πολλῶν προφητῶν ἐκηρύχθη
lit: Many things therefore, and other things, were proclaimed by many prophets
sm: Many other things were proclaimed by many prophets

syn.mc: Main clause summarizing the prophetic witness.
syn.svo: Neuter plural subject: πολλὰ ... ἕτερα. Verb: ἐκηρύχθη. Agent phrase: ὑπὸ πολλῶν προφητῶν.
syn.rhet: The summary broadens from named citations to a wider prophetic chorus.

src: εἰς τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον,
lit: Into the mystery of the Passover,
sm: into the mystery of the Passover,

syn.mc: Prepositional phrase completing the clause of line 481.
syn.svo: Prepositional complement of ἐκηρύχθη: εἰς τὸ ... μυστήριον.
syn.rhet: The prophetic witness is gathered into a single paschal mystery.

src: ὅ ἐστιν Χριστός"
lit: Which is Christ
sm: which is Christ
emd: Removed trailing OCR quotation mark; no lexical change.

syn.mc: Relative predication identifying the mystery.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause beyond the relative frame.
syn.svo: Relative subject: ὅ. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate nominative: Χριστός.
syn.rhet: The sacrificial mystery is explicitly christological.

src: ᾧ ἡ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας. ἀμήν.
lit: To whom [be] the glory unto the ages. Amen.
sm: To him be glory unto the ages. Amen.

syn.mc: Elliptical doxological clause.
syn.sub: The relative pronoun links back to Christ.
syn.svo: Dative referent: ᾧ. Subject: ἡ δόξα. Implied predicate such as ἔστω or ἐστίν. Prepositional phrase: εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας.
syn.rhet: A conventional doxology seals the citation block.

src: Οὗτος ἀφικόμενος ἐξ οὐρανῶν ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν διὰ τὸν πάσχοντα,
tok: aor mid ptcp nom sg m
pres act ptcp acc sg m
lit: This one, having come from heaven to earth for the sufferer,
sm: This one, having come from heaven to earth for the sufferer,

syn.mc: Opening of the final christological sentence.
syn.sub: The participle ἀφικόμενος is circumstantial and leads into the main verbs of the following lines.
syn.svo: Subject: Οὗτος. Participles: ἀφικόμενος. Prepositional phrases: ἐξ οὐρανῶν, ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν, διὰ τὸν πάσχοντα.
syn.ptc: ἀφικόμενος frames the movement from heaven to earth.
syn.rhet: The section pivots from quotation to direct christological exposition.

src: αὐτὸν δὲ ἐκεῖνον ἐνδυσάμενος διὰ παρθένου μήτρας
tok: aor mid ptcp nom sg m
lit: And having clothed himself with him, that one, through a virgin womb
sm: and having clothed himself with that one through a virgin womb,

syn.mc: Participial continuation of the christological sentence.
syn.svo: Objects of ἐνδυσάμενος: αὐτὸν ἐκεῖνον. Prepositional phrase: διὰ παρθένου μήτρας.
syn.ptc: ἐνδυσάμενος is circumstantial and continues the movement of incarnation.
syn.rhet: The incarnation is described as taking on the other person through virginal birth.

src: καὶ προελθὼν ἄνθρωπος,
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: And having come forth as a man,
sm: and coming forth as a man,

syn.mc: Participial continuation of the same sentence.
syn.svo: Participle: προελθών. Noun ἄνθρωπος functions appositionally with Οὗτος from line 485.
syn.ptc: προελθὼν is circumstantial and the nominal ἄνθρωπος is appositive/predicative.
syn.app: ἄνθρωπος apposes the subject and states the mode of appearance.
syn.rhet: The descent from heaven is completed as full humanity.

src: ἀπεδέξατο τὰ τοῦ πάσχοντος πάθη
tok: pres act ptcp gen sg m
lit: He received the sufferings of the one suffering
sm: He took on the sufferings of the sufferer

syn.mc: Main finite verb of the christological exposition.
syn.svo: Subject: Οὗτος from line 485. Verb: ἀπεδέξατο. Object: τὰ ... πάθη. Genitive modifier: τοῦ πάσχοντος.
syn.ptc: πάσχοντος is substantival, defining whose sufferings are in view.
syn.rhet: The exposition moves from coming and clothing to assuming suffering itself.

src: διὰ τοῦ παθεῖν δυναμένου σώματος,
tok: pres mp ptcp gen sg n
lit: through the body capable of suffering,
sm: through the body capable of suffering,

syn.mc: Prepositional phrase completing the object of line 488.
syn.sub: The articular infinitive τοῦ παθεῖν is governed by διὰ.
syn.svo: No finite verb. Genitive articular infinitive phrase: τοῦ παθεῖν. Participial modifier: δυναμένου. Head noun: σώματος.
syn.ptc: δυναμένου qualifies σώματος and states capacity for suffering.
syn.rhet: The embodied means of suffering is made explicit.

src: καὶ κατέλυσεν τὰ τῆς σαρκὸς πάθη"
ana: καὶ κατέλυσεν τὰ τῆς σαρκὸς πάθη
lit: And he abolished the passions of the flesh
sm: And he abolished the passions of the flesh
emd: Removed trailing OCR quotation mark; no lexical change.

syn.mc: Final coordinated finite clause.
syn.svo: Subject: Οὗτος from line 485. Verb: κατέλυσεν. Object: τὰ ... πάθη. Genitive modifier: τῆς σαρκὸς.
syn.rhet: The sentence closes by contrasting the sufferer’s assumed pain with the destruction of fleshly passions.
